item.title: Song of the United Armies
item.description: Song of the United Armies (Russian: Песня объединённых армий; Romanized: Pesnya ob Yedinonnykh Armiy) was a military march used as the anthem of the Warsaw Pact,[1] written in 1958.[2]

item.title: Meshchyora, Sobinsky District, Vladimir Oblast
item.description: Meshchyora (Russian: Мещёра) is a rural locality (a village) in Aserkhovskoye Rural Settlement, Sobinsky District, Vladimir Oblast, Russia. The population was 1 as of 2010.[2]

item.title: Igor Taran
item.description: Igor Taran (Russian: Игорь Таран) (born 29 December 1983 in G'uzor) is an Uzbekistani footballer who plays as a striker. He currently plays for Neftchi Fergana.

item.title: Armando J. Parodi
item.description: Armando J. Parodi (born March 16, 1942) is an Argentine glycobiologist. He did his initial education at the School of Sciences of the University of Buenos Aires. His PhD work was done under Luis Federico Leloir, a recipient of the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for his work involving the finding of sugar nucleotides and how they play a role in the making of oligosaccharides and polysaccharides. He also pursued postdoc work at the Pasteur Institute in Paris, France and Duke University in Durham, NC, USA.

item.title: David Lindsay (Scottish footballer)
item.description: David Lindsay was a Scottish footballer who played as a right winger.
